input.topic: A feature about ransomware attacks on hospitals, written for a general newspaper audience
input.article_excerpt: The attackers used a zero-day exploit to gain initial access, then deployed ransomware across the hospital's network via lateral movement. The hospital's EDR solution failed to detect the payload because the malware used fileless techniques. Negotiations with the threat actor were conducted through a Tor-based communication channel. The hospital ultimately paid a ransom of 40 Bitcoin through a cryptocurrency tumbler.

input.topic: A feature about the EU's MiCA regulation of cryptocurrency markets, written for an informed European audience
input.article_excerpt: The Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation requires all crypto-asset service providers to obtain authorisation from a national competent authority before operating in the single market. Stablecoin issuers must maintain adequate reserves and submit to regular audits. The regulation introduces passporting rights, allowing firms authorised in one member state to operate across all 27 without additional licences.
